page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.large is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, 4.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core, and no local storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a significant divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, ranks in the top tier with a score of 3111.68. However, multi-core CPU performance drops to the bottom 25% with a score of 3113.59, indicating limited scaling across its two cores. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is weak at 153.15 GB/sec, large-block read and write bandwidths perform in the average tier at 47.32 GB/sec and 27.49 GB/sec respectively. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also perform in the average range.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is classified as compute-optimized. It lacks local storage and a GPU, which limits its utility for data-heavy storage or accelerated graphics workloads. Passmark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks show weak performance, placing them in the bottom 25%. Consequently, the ecs.c8ae.large is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded compute tasks, basic web serving, or utility workloads that benefit from high single-core CPU speeds but do not require extensive memory or multi-threaded scaling.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor at 3.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM and no local storage, this KVM-based server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Performance benchmarks highlight an exceptional single-core CPU capability, ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. However, multi-core scaling and database operations fall into the lower performance tiers. With its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance represents a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring strong single-threaded execution, such as basic web serving, lightweight single-threaded processing, and utility tasks, while avoiding the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.large is a compute-optimized KVM instance with 2 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high single-core performance for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated AMD EPYC 9T34 vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic web serving
